perturbation.original_text: 2. (a) Carrier shall invoice Shipper for the services provided under this contract promptly upon performance. All sums due under any invoice shall be payable without discount upon receipt of the invoice. Amounts more than 30 days past due shall bear interest from the due date to the date of payment at the lesser of 1) the rate of interest established by Chase Manhattan Bank, New York, NY, from time to time as its prime rate, plus two (2) percent, or 2) the maximum non-usurious interest rate which may be charged Shipper pursuant to applicable Texas law, Article 5069-1.04 Texas Rev. Civ. Stat., as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. (a) Carrier shall invoice Shipper for the services provided under this contract promptly upon performance. All sums due under any invoice shall be payable without discount within 15 days of receipt of the invoice. Amounts more than 30 days past due shall bear interest from the due date to the date of payment at the lesser of 1) the rate of interest established by Chase Manhattan Bank, New York, NY, from time to time as its prime rate, plus two (2) percent, or 2) the maximum non-usurious interest rate which may be charged Shipper pursuant to applicable Texas law, Article 5069-1.04 Texas Rev. Civ. Stat., as amended.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the payment terms. The original text states invoices are payable upon receipt, while the modified text specifies payment must be made within 15 days of receipt. This creates uncertainty about when payment is actually due. The term 'without discount' further contributes to the ambiguity because it conflicts the modified payment time frame of 15 days of receipt, creating further question in when should a payment be due.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. (a) Carrier shall invoice Shipper for the services provided under this contract promptly upon performance. All sums due under any invoice shall be payable without discount within 15 days of receipt of the invoice. Amounts more than 30 days past due shall bear interest from the due date to the date of payment at the lesser of 1) the rate of interest established by Chase Manhattan Bank, New York, NY, from time to time as its prime rate, plus two (2) percent, or 2) the maximum non-usurious interest rate which may be charged Shipper pursuant to applicable Texas law, Article 5069-1.04 Texas Rev. Civ. Stat., as amended.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the payment terms from 'upon receipt' to 'within 15 days of receipt' while retaining the 'without discount' clause. This creates a direct conflict regarding when payment is due, as it's unclear if payment is expected immediately upon receipt or allowed within 15 days, especially with the presence of 'without discount' and thus qualifies as a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The terms and conditions of this contract and all information concerning the business, customers, products, and processes of each party which may come into the possession of the other party during the course of the negotiation or performance of this contract are confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party; provided, however, either party may disclose information concerning this contract to any independent public accounting firm retained to perform an annual financial audit of that party. This obligation of confidentiality shall expire two (2) years from the date of the last shipment under this contract.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The terms and conditions of this contract and all information concerning the business, customers, products, and processes of each party which may come into the possession of the other party during the course of the negotiation or performance of this contract are confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party; provided, however, either party may disclose information concerning this contract to any independent public accounting firm retained to perform an annual financial audit of that party. This obligation of confidentiality shall automatically expire upon termination of this contract.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the confidentiality terms' expiration. The original text specifies the confidentiality obligation expires two years after the last shipment, while the modified text states it expires upon contract termination. This creates uncertainty about the duration of the confidentiality obligation. This conflicts with the sentence that speaks about the two years from the date of the last shipment, creating further ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. The terms and conditions of this contract and all information concerning the business, customers, products, and processes of each party which may come into the possession of the other party during the course of the negotiation or performance of this contract are confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party; provided, however, either party may disclose information concerning this contract to any independent public accounting firm retained to perform an annual financial audit of that party. This obligation of confidentiality shall automatically expire upon termination of this contract.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the confidentiality obligation expires two years after the last shipment, while the modified text states it expires upon contract termination. This introduces an inconsistency regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation. The contradicted text is the same section as the changed text, further creating ambiguity as the termination of the contract may happen before the last shipment creating a contradiction. This falls under the definition of inconsistencies - in text contradiction, where different sections of a contract (in this case the same section) provide conflicting deadlines or obligations. Thus, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
